channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ted Turner, Founder of CNN, Passes Away at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the visionary media mogul and founder of the Cable News Network (CNN), has passed away at the age of 87, CNN confirmed on Wednesday. Turner, widely recognised for revolutionizing news broa...

item.title: Oricred Establishes Working Capital Hub in Mokopane to Support Local Entrepreneurs
item.description: Oricred has established a purpose-built working capital Hub in Mokopane, aiming to close the gap between funding and execution for entrepreneurs operating in South Africa’s platinum belt. Many local b...

item.title: University of Ibadan Students Protest Over Ongoing Strike and Collapse of Basic Services
item.description: Serious tension show for University of Ibadan on May 6, 2026, as students come out protest because of worsening living and learning conditions for campus. The protest start small from students wey dey...

item.title: Thursday’s Superquiz and Mini Crossword for Readers
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald's Superquiz for Thursday, 7 May, offers readers a mix of brain-teasing fun and interactive engagement. The feature includes a Mini Crossword comprising ten clues spread acros...

item.title: U.S. corporate earnings surge, supporting stock market gains
item.description: U.S. corporate earnings have surged in the first quarter, propelling the stock market to new record highs. More than two-thirds of S&P 500 companies have reported results, indicating their highest qua...

item.title: Analyzing the Toboso Incident and Its Implications
item.description: The recent deadly encounters in Toboso, Negros Occidental on April 19, which resulted in 19 deaths including nine civilians, have sparked urgent calls for accountability. The incident has raised criti...

item.title: Myanmar President Will Not Attend ASEAN Summit 2026; Other Member States to Participate
item.description: The ASEAN Summit 2026 in Cebu will be attended by all heads of state from member countries except Myanmar. According to Dominic Xavier Imperial, Foreign Affairs Assistant Secretary and ASEAN spokesman...

item.title: Premium cinema experiences drive ticket prices as attendance declines
item.description: Movie ticket prices in the United States have reached unprecedented levels, with some premium screenings costing up to $50 per ticket. The rise in cost reflects the increasing investment by cinema ope...

item.title: Royal Commission Examines Violent Antisemitism Against Jewish Australians
item.description: The royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion has heard disturbing evidence about the prevalence of antisemitic behaviour in Australia. Jewish political candidate Joshua Kirsh described rec...

item.title: PIC reports R350bn drop in assets following US-Iran conflict
item.description: The Public Investment Corporation (PIC) has reported a significant reduction of nearly R350 billion in its assets under management as a direct consequence of the ongoing US-Iran conflict. PIC CEO Patr...

item.title: China criticises Eswatini for hosting Taiwan President Lai Ching-te
item.description: China has publicly criticised Eswatini for hosting Taiwan President Lai Ching-te, accusing the small Southern African kingdom of being 'kept and fed' by Taiwan. The remarks were unusually strong, high...

item.title: Banks Hesitate to Finance Small-Scale Mining Due to Data Gaps and High Risks
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Banks in Tanzania are cautious when it comes to lending to small and medium-sized mining projects. High risks, limited collateral, and poor data make financiers wary. Unlike large-scal...

item.title: Government Praises CRDB-UDSM Collaboration for Boosting Innovation and Entrepreneurship
item.description: The Tanzanian government has praised the collaboration between CRDB Bank Foundation and the University of Dar es Salaam (UDSM) for its important role in promoting innovation and entrepreneurship among...

item.title: Yosemite National Park Faces Traffic and Overcrowding After Ending Reservation System
item.description: Yosemite National Park, California’s most visited national park, is experiencing unprecedented crowds, with numbers reaching the highest in a decade. Over the past weekend, visitors reported waiting u...

item.title: Majority of Gold Medals at Kuvempu University Convocation Won by Female Students
item.description: At the 35th annual convocation of Kuvempu University, held at Shankaraghatta near Shivamogga, female students dominated the awards. Out of 79 gold medallists, 68 were girls, and of the 29 students rec...

item.title: La Salle Lady Spikers take early lead in UAAP Season 88 finals against NU
item.description: The La Salle Lady Spikers displayed exceptional form in Game 1 of the UAAP Season 88 women's volleyball finals, defeating the two-time defending champions NU Lady Bulldogs in straight sets, 25-23, 25-...

item.title: Experts and Leaders Discuss Climate Change Challenges on Pakistan's Agriculture Sector
item.description: The second Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference took place in Islamabad, bringing together business leaders, policymakers, and climate experts to deliberate on the pressing challen...

item.title: Protesters disrupt Russian pavilion at Venice Biennale over Ukraine war
item.description: At the Venice Biennale, tensions flared as protesters blockaded the Russian pavilion in opposition to the war in Ukraine. Activist groups, including Russian collective Pussy Riot and Ukrainian members...

item.title: Punjab Political Leaders from Congress and BJP Criticise Bhagwant Mann's Statement on Blast
item.description: The news concerns a recent political disagreement in Punjab where both Congress and BJP leaders expressed their criticism of Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann's comments regarding a blast incident. While t...

item.title: Delay in Employment of 774 National Health Fellows Sparks Concern Over Government Commitment
item.description: More than one year after President Bola Tinubu promise automatic employment to 774 National Health Fellows, the beneficiaries say government never fulfil that pledge, leaving many of them stranded and...
